_source: Extracted verbatim from manning_horton_2026_source/optimize.tex (line 644-645, 621) and tables/personas.tex
_cross_check: Weights verified against paper_key_details.md section 3 — exact match confirmed
system_preamble: You are a human being with all the cognitive biases and heuristics that come with it.
klevel_explanation: A k-level thinker thinks k steps ahead. A 0-level thinker thinks 0 steps and would, therefore, just select the maximum amount that guarantees money.
notes_on_preamble: The system_preamble is prepended to ALL 10 strategic personas. The klevel_explanation is additionally included for all personas EXCEPT the random-picker persona (9th in list). Source: optimize.tex footnote to Table 2, lines 644-645.
chain_of_thought_prompt_1: {11-20 game instructions}. Reason out a few settings according to your personality and how others might respond.
chain_of_thought_prompt_2: {11-20 game instructions}. You previously had the following thoughts: {Response to prompt 1}. What amount of money would you request?
notes_on_cot: Two sequential prompts implement chain-of-thought reasoning (Wei et al. 2024). The placeholder {11-20 game instructions} is the full game instruction text verbatim. Source: optimize.tex line 621 (footnote to the elicitation paragraph). Each persona was elicited 100 times with GPT-4o at temperature=1.
game_instructions_basic_1120: You and another player are playing a game in which each player requests an amount of money. The amount must be (an integer) between 11 and 20 shekels. Each player will receive the amount he requests. A player will receive an additional amount of 20 shekels if he asks for exactly one shekel less than the other player. What amount of money would you request?

personas.name: L0 thinker
personas.prompt_text: You are generally a 0-level thinker---picking the option with the most guaranteed money.

personas.name: L0-L1 thinker
personas.prompt_text: You vary between a 0 and 1-level thinker.

personas.name: L1-L2 thinker
personas.prompt_text: You vary between a 1 and 2-level thinker.

personas.name: L0-L1-L2 thinker
personas.prompt_text: You vary between a 0, 1, and 2-level thinker.

personas.name: L0-L1-L2-L3 thinker
personas.prompt_text: You vary between a 0, 1, 2, and 3-level thinker.

personas.name: L1-L2-L3 thinker
personas.prompt_text: You vary between a 1, 2, and 3-level thinker.
personas.notes: Largest weight: 46.9% of optimized mixture. Corresponds to L3 play (choose 17) as modal output.

personas.name: L0-L1-L2-L3-L4 thinker
personas.prompt_text: You vary between a 0, 1, 2, 3, and 4-level thinker.

personas.name: L0-L1-L2-L3-L4-L5 thinker
personas.prompt_text: You vary between a 0, 1, 2, 3, 4 and 5-level thinker.
personas.notes: Second largest weight: 33.9% of optimized mixture

personas.name: Random lower picker
personas.prompt_text: You randomly pick between lower numbers because you think that's the best way to win.
personas.notes: The ONLY persona that does NOT receive the k-level explanation. Third largest weight.

full_prompt_assembly_for_non_random_pers.description: The complete prompt sent as the SYSTEM prompt or prepended context for each agent (all except the random-picker persona)
full_prompt_assembly_for_non_random_pers.example_for_persona_6: You are a human being with all the cognitive biases and heuristics that come with it. A k-level thinker thinks k steps ahead. A 0-level thinker thinks 0 steps and would, therefore, just select the maximum amount that guarantees money. You vary between a 1, 2, and 3-level thinker.

full_prompt_assembly_for_random_persona.description: The complete prompt for the random-picker persona (no k-level explanation)
full_prompt_assembly_for_random_persona.example: You are a human being with all the cognitive biases and heuristics that come with it. You randomly pick between lower numbers because you think that's the best way to win.

weight_verification.discrepancies: None. Both sources agree exactly on all 10 weights.

optimization_method.objective: Minimize absolute difference between CDFs of human distribution P and mixture distribution
optimization_method.training_data: Basic 11-20 game, n=108 human observations (Arad & Rubinstein 2012)

atheoretical_personas.note: Three atheoretical candidate sets used as negative controls (Table A1 / bad_personas.tex). All failed validation.

always_pick_n.note: This set achieved perfect in-sample fit (by construction) but completely failed validation.
